event: Courses of both pregnancy and vaginal delivery were uncomplicated
No prior history of head injury
Computed tomography scan showed grade 2 subarachnoid hemorrhage
Digital subtraction angiography revealed ruptured right middle cerebral artery aneurysm measuring 3.5 mm
Treated with percutaneous endovascular coil embolization
Admitted again with suspected postpartum endometritis
Initiation of empiric antibacterial therapy (piperacillin/tazobactam, vancomycin, azithromycin)
Transferred to intensive care unit of university hospital
Invasive blood pressure 90/65 mmHg requiring crystalloid and vasopressor
Coarse rales over lower lung zones
Sequential organ failure assessment score of 4
Blood cultures showed E. coli sensitive to piperacillin/tazobactam
Chest radiography showed pulmonary nodules up to 40 mm
Computed tomography of thorax confirmed pulmonary nodules
Transvaginal ultrasound depicted enlarged uterus with complete loss of zonal anatomy
Magnetic resonance imaging confirmed enlarged heterogeneous myometrial mass
Magnetic resonance imaging confirmed single splenic metastatic lesion
Commenced low-dose etoposide in combination with cisplatin
Experienced grade 3 neutropenia after second cycle of EMA/CO
Required incorporation of granulocyte colony stimulating factor
Dose reduction of etoposide and actinomycin D
Suffered grade 2 nausea controlled with dexamethasone and ondansetron
Restaging MRI and PET/CT indicated decreased pulmonary nodules and uterine mass
Normalization of β-hCG within a month after completion of three cycles of EP/EMA
Recommended use of male condoms to prevent unplanned pregnancy
Patient remains free of disease for almost two years
